authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's preventive-controls rule page is an authoritative public baseline for facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food and it defines monitoring, corrective-action, supply-chain, and recall-plan expectations that brand operations must respect.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: brand-SKU readiness, in-process execution control, and corrective-action boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for cGMP, warehousing, distribution, records, corrective actions, and supply-chain programs that constrain CPG brand execution work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: production execution, warehousing, records posture, and release-boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Industry: Supply-Chain Program Requirements and Co-Manufacturer Supplier Approval and Verification for Human Food and Animal Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA guidance directly addresses brand-owner, receiving-facility, and co-manufacturer supplier approval and verification boundaries in packaged-food supply chains.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: supplier verification, co-manufacturer coordination, and substitution-boundary handling
used_for: handoff boundary between execution support and final supplier approval

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Proposes to Extend Compliance Date for Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This FDA constituent update dated August 6, 2025 is the current public agency statement on Food Traceability Rule timing and notes the proposed extension to July 20, 2028, which is material when execution posture depends on date-sensitive traceability controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: date-sensitive traceability handling and deployment caveats
used_for: tenant confirmation trigger for covered products

authoritative_sources.title: Food Traceability List
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA maintains the public list of higher-risk foods subject to additional traceability records, which is authoritative for covered-food scope and lot-record expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for when finished goods must move from normal execution into recall, hold, or formal quality-led disposition.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hold, blocked release, and recall-trigger escalation
used_for: refusal to continue execution under recall conditions

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized public standards body for identifier and traceability exchange standards used across CPG supplier, plant, warehouse, and retailer workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capture and reconciliation of item, lot, location, and event data

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Data Synchronisation Network (GDSN)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GDSN is the recognized public standard for synchronized item master data used by brand owners and trading partners to keep pack and attribute records aligned.

authoritative_sources.title: GTIN Management Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GTIN Management Standard is the authoritative public baseline for deciding when product or pack changes require new trade-item identifiers and downstream system updates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: brand-SKU identity control and change-trigger review

authoritative_sources.title: How to Conduct a Recall
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC recall guidance is authoritative for U.S. non-food consumer goods and helps define where this lane must escalate into non-food recall handling rather than assuming a food-only baseline.

workflow_stages.name: brand intake and readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Review brand SKU, plant or co-manufacturer assignment, order status, lot references, constrained materials, and release prerequisites to determine whether work can begin or continue.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: in-process execution and throughput exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track execution state, throughput, nonconformances, holds, and unresolved dependencies while keeping release and approval authority explicit.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: supplier, co-manufacturer, and item-record reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Validate approved suppliers, item-master records, co-manufacturer output, and substitution boundaries before advancement is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: inventory, lot, and traceability reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that batch, lot, finished-goods inventory, warehouse status, and partner records reconcile before release-dependent progression or completion is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and escalation packaging
workflow_stages.description: Close work only when reconciliation, signoff, and release-dependent evidence are present, and escalate holds, recalls, substitutions, and disposition questions rather than absorbing those approvals.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: production orders, brand SKU priorities, and plant or co-manufacturer assignments
batch records, lot genealogy, finished-goods inventory states, and warehouse transactions
approved supplier lists, co-manufacturer records, substitution requests, and supplier-quality findings
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, deviation records, and rework authorizations
item-master records, GTIN mappings, and trading-partner product data references
completion checklists, signoff records, exception notes, and escalation packets
tenant authority maps, service-level commitments, and change-control documentation

allowed: classify brand operations work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, approved supplier records, lot context, and system-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: final engineering, formulation, or process-change approval
final supplier approval, substitution approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
final QA release, safety override, recall disposition, or destruction decision
legal, regulatory, or product-category interpretation when public guidance and tenant policy conflict
refuse: start or continue work when authoritative lot, inventory, supplier, or release evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark work complete without reconciliation, inventory, lot, or signoff evidence
bypass traceability, release, hold, or quality controls to protect brand service level or commercial commitments

domain_failure_modes: co-manufacturer, ERP, and WMS records conflict on lot identity or finished-goods quantity
brand work is declared complete without reconciliation, inventory update, or required signoff evidence
quality hold, recall trigger, or nonconformance is treated as a local service-level problem instead of a formal release-boundary event
supplier substitutions are treated as routine scheduling decisions instead of supplier-quality and change-control escalations
item-master or GTIN changes are treated as operational shortcuts instead of identity-control issues requiring external approval
the lane drifts into engineering, supplier approval, legal interpretation, or final release authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-food CPG operating overlays outside FDA food-specific scope
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The validated baseline is anchored in FDA food-oriented CPG operations plus GS1 and CPSC public references. Tenants covering cosmetics, OTC drugs, or other product-specific regimes must supply the applicable overlays at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific release and rework authority maps
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define hold, supplier, traceability, and recall boundaries, but each tenant still needs plant-specific SOPs and authority maps to decide whether rework, relabel, return, or destruction paths are permitted.

notes: Authoritative batch, lot, inventory, item-master, supplier, and release records are the ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier-management, and product-data systems designated by the tenant, constrained by FDA, CPSC, and GS1 baselines where applicable.
The lane stops before formal quality release, engineering change approval, supplier approval, and recall disposition even when customer-ready inventory or commercial commitments are under pressure.
